MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-08-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS TAK TSUI, TAK TSUI MORTGAGE, INC. AND GRACE LAW'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Plaintiff failed to shift the burden of producing evidence because plaintiff failed to negate Chan's testimony as to when he learned of Tsui's and Law's conduct. (defendant's facts 5, 7, 9, 14). The motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the 7th cause of action because unjust enrichment is a remedy not a cause of action. It is denied as to the other causes of action. The burden did not shift as the 1st, 2nd, 4th, 5th and 8th causes of action. (defendant's facts 5, 7, 9, 14, 24, 25, 26, 33, 34, 35). There are triable issues of fact as to the third and sixth causes of action (defendant's facts 5, 7, 9, 14, 19, 20, 24, 25, 26, 40; plaintiff's facts 2, 3, 5). Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/VC) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

Off Calendar May-25-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff Lendint Team Morgage's motion to compel production of documents is granted. The objections are overruled. Defendant is ordered to produce all responsive documents within 15 days of notice of this order. Defendant may redact social security numbers of third parties that appear on any documents. Counsel for plaintiff may not disclose or permit to be disclosed the information described in the records unless specifically permitted to do so by the court after a hearing and a showing of good cause. Notwithstanding the above, counsel for plaintiff may disclose or permit to be disclosed the information in the records to persons employed by counsel to assist in the preparation of the case, if that disclosure is required for that preparation. Persons provided this information by counsel shall be informed that further dissemination of the information constitutes a violation of a court order. The Court finds that defendant asserted unmeritorious objections and failed to make a reasonable and good faith effort to informally resolve the matters in dispute. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $2100 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.
